question: Based on the stacked bar chart showing performance indicators, which quarter appears to have the best overall performance?

options.A: Q1 shows the best performance because it has the highest stacked total.
options.B: Q2 shows the best performance because it has the highest stacked total.
options.C: Q3 shows the best performance because it has balanced metrics across all categories.
options.D: The chart cannot determine overall performance accurately because it combines unrelated metrics that cannot be meaningfully added together.

explanation: The chart stacks unrelated metrics (Store Visits, Electricity Consumption, etc.) into a single total. These metrics have different units and meanings (e.g., counts, temperatures, consumption), so adding them together creates a meaningless sum. The stacked height cannot be used to assess overall performance. Only individual metric comparisons are valid.
